chunk_summary: Anemia and iron deficiency contribute to stunting by impairing appetite, immunity, and neurodevelopment. India's Anaemia Mukt Bharat strategy and WIFS program address anemia across life stages, emphasizing dietary optimization, deworming, and adherence to supplementation protocols.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of anemia and iron deficiency in chronic growth faltering and stunting.
Learn the key components of India's Anaemia Mukt Bharat (AMB) strategy and WIFS program.
Identify clinical approaches to managing anemia in stunted children, including diagnostics and dietary interventions.
Recognize the importance of addressing adherence barriers in anemia treatment.
key_facts: Anemia is both a marker and driver of stunting, impairing appetite, immunity, and neurodevelopment.
India’s AMB strategy targets children 6–59 months, school-age children, adolescents, and pregnant/lactating women.
WIFS program provides weekly iron and folic acid supplementation for adolescents, linked to school and menstrual health.
First-line anemia evaluation includes CBC with indices and ferritin, interpreted in the context of inflammation.
Helminth treatment follows national schedules to address persistent infections contributing to anemia.
Adherence barriers, such as missed doses and side effects, are common reasons for persistent anemia.
reference_quote: India’s AMB (Anaemia Mukt Bharat) strategy provides a lifecycle dosing platform that can be operationalized in pediatric notes.
document_key_topics: Role of anemia and iron deficiency in stunting
Clinical management of anemia in stunted children
